title: Kaiming He（Fellow of the American National Academy of Artificial Intelligence, Associate Professor in the MIT Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, and recipient of the Future Science Prize.）_Baiduwiki
text:
English

Key Information

Kaiming He

Fellow of the American National Academy of Artificial Intelligence, Associate Professor in the MIT Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, and recipient of the Future Science Prize.

The most cited AI paper of the 21st century was written by this Chinese genius(from:量子位)

12:11

Kaiming He, born in 1984 in Guangzhou, Guangdong, is an artificial intelligence scientist, a Fellow of the American National Academy of Artificial Intelligence, an associate professor (with tenure) and the Douglas Ross Software Technology Career Development Professor in the MIT Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, and concurrently serves as a Distinguished Scientist at Google DeepMind.

1

Kaiming He graduated from Zhixin High School in 2003, achieving the top score in the Guangdong province college entrance examination with a standard score of 900. From 2003 to 2007, he studied in the Tsinghua University Physics Department Basic Science Class, receiving the Tsinghua Scholarship for three consecutive years. After completing his undergraduate studies, he pursued graduate studies at The Chinese University of Hong Kong under the supervision of TANG Xiaoou, Sean . In 2009, he became the first Chinese recipient of the CVPR Best Paper Award. He obtained his Ph.D. in Information Engineering from The Chinese University of Hong Kong in 2011 and subsequently worked at Microsoft Research Asia. In August 2016, he joined Facebook AI Research (FAIR) as a research scientist. He received the ICCV Best Paper Award (Marr Prize) in 2017 and the PAMI Young Researcher Award at the CVPR conference in 2018. In 2022, he was listed on the AI 2000 Most Influential Scholars in Artificial Intelligence List, ranking first overall. In 2023, he received the Future Science Prize in Mathematics and Computer Science. In February 2024, he joined the MIT Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science as an associate professor; in July of the same year, he was appointed the Douglas Ross Software Technology Career Development Professor. In June 2025, he was granted tenure at MIT; in the same year, he was elected a Fellow of the American National Academy of Artificial Intelligence.

1

Kaiming He's primary research areas are Computer Vision and Deep Learning. The concept of the Deep Residual Network (ResNet) he proposed demonstrated that training networks with hundreds of layers is entirely feasible, representing groundbreaking work in the field of Deep Learning.

2

Personal Experience

Early Life

Kaiming He was born in 1984 and grew up in Guangzhou. While attending Guangzhou Zhixin High School, he won the National Physics Competition First Prize and the Guangdong Province Chemistry Competition First Prize.

In May 2003, he was admitted to Tsinghua University's Mechanical Engineering and Automation program without taking the national college entrance exam, based on his National Physics Competition First Prize. In June, he chose to take the exam nonetheless and achieved a standardized score of 900, becoming one of the nine top scorers in Guangdong Province that year.

20

In September 2003, after entering Tsinghua University, Kaiming He gave up his originally guaranteed major and instead chose the Basic Science Class. During his undergraduate studies, he received the Tsinghua Scholarship for three consecutive years.

In 2007, before graduation, Kaiming He began an internship at Microsoft Research Asia (MSRA). Due to his interest in computer graphics and image courses, he chose to join the Visual Computing Group at MSRA, with Jian Sun as his internship mentor. After completing his bachelor's degree, he pursued graduate studies at The Chinese University of Hong Kong, under the supervision of TANG Xiaoou, Sean, while continuing to participate in related research at Microsoft Research Asia.

1

In 2009, his first research paper received the Best Paper Award at the IEEE Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR), marking the first time in CVPR's 25-year history that a researcher of Chinese or Asian origin had won the award.

Kaiming He (right third) receiving the 2009 Microsoft Fellowship

Kaiming He

Professional Career

In 2011, Kaiming He graduated from the Multimedia Laboratory at The Chinese University of Hong Kong with a PhD in Information Engineering and subsequently began working full-time at Microsoft Research Asia.

25

In 2015, Kaiming He and his team, using the 152-layer Deep Residual Network ResNet-152, defeated teams from industry giants like Google, Intel, and Qualcomm to win first place in the ImageNet Large Scale Visual Recognition Challenge.

In August 2016, he left Microsoft Research Asia to join Facebook AI Research (FAIR) as a Research Scientist.

1

In August 2023, he received the Future Science Prize in Mathematics and Computer Science, with a prize of $333,000. In October of the same year, he delivered his award lecture titled "Deep Residual Learning and Its Impact on the Field of Artificial Intelligence" at The Chinese University of Hong Kong.

21

In February 2024, he joined the MIT Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science (MIT EECS) as an Associate Professor. In July of the same year, he was appointed the Douglas Ross Software Technology Career Development Professor.

26

In June 2025, he was granted MIT Tenure and joined DeepMind, an AI research organization under Google, holding the position of "Part-time Distinguished Scientist".

34

Major Achievements

Scientific Research Achievements

* Research Overview

The team led by Kaiming He proposed Deep Residual Learning, which enabled neural networks to achieve unprecedented depth and gain abilities previously difficult to attain, contributing to several breakthrough achievements—including AlphaGo, AlphaFold, and ChatGPT—making fundamental contributions to Artificial Intelligence.

22

In March 2017, Kaiming He and his colleagues published Mask R-CNN, introducing a conceptually simple, flexible, and general framework for Object Instance Segmentation, capable of effectively detecting objects within images while simultaneously generating a high-quality segmentation mask for each instance.

1

Kaiming He (right) with his advisor, Professor Xiaoou Tang (left)

Talent Development

In the Spring of 2024, Kaiming He taught the course "Advances in Computer Vision" at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology.

29

Kaiming He teaching at MIT

Major Works

According to Google Scholar statistics, as of July 2023, Kaiming He has published 73 papers, with an H-index of 67 and over 460,000 citations.

20

Representative Papers:

He, K., Chen, X., Xie, S., Li, Y., Dollár, P., & Girshick, R. (2022). Masked autoencoders are scalable vision learners. In Proceedings of the IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (pp. 16000-16009).

9

Chen, X., & He, K. (2021). Exploring simple siamese representation learning. In Proceedings of the IEEE/CVF conference on computer vision and pattern recognition (pp. 15750-15758).

10

He, K., Fan, H., Wu, Y., Xie, S., & Girshick, R. (2020). Momentum contrast for unsupervised visual representation learning. In Proceedings of the IEEE/CVF conference on computer vision and pattern recognition (pp. 9729-9738).

11

Qi, C. R., Litany, O., He, K., & Guibas, L. J. (2019). Deep hough voting for 3d object detection in point clouds. In proceedings of the IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision (pp. 9277-9286).

12

Wu, Y., & He, K. (2018). Group normalization. In Proceedings of the European conference on computer vision (ECCV) (pp. 3-19).

13

He, K., Gkioxari, G., Dollár, P., & Girshick, R. (2017). Mask r-cnn. In Proceedings of the IEEE international conference on computer vision (pp. 2961-2969).

14

Lin, T. Y., Goyal, P., Girshick, R., He, K., & Dollár, P. (2017). Focal loss for dense object detection. In Proceedings of the IEEE international conference on computer vision (pp. 2980-2988).

15

He, K., Zhang, X., Ren, S., & Sun, J. (2016). Deep residual learning for image recognition. In Proceedings of the IEEE conference on computer vision and pattern recognition (pp. 770-778).

16

He, K., Sun, J., & Tang, X. (2010). Single image haze removal using dark channel prior. IEEE transactions on pattern analysis and machine intelligence, 33(12), 2341-2353.

17

Personal Life

* Family Background

Kaiming He is the only child in his family. Both of his parents work in management positions at enterprises, and he was exposed to a high-quality educational environment from a young age. In his youth, he was sent to the Children's Palace to learn painting, often staying for half a day or longer, which gradually cultivated a calm and steady temperament in him.

1

Evaluation

When mentioning Kaiming He, many in academia regard him as a "genius-type" figure. From being a "National College Entrance Examination top scorer with a perfect score," to the "first Chinese recipient" of the CVPR Best Paper Award, and then to the groundbreaking "Deep Residual Network" that astonished the academic world, this talented young scholar from the "post-80s generation" has many legendary stories. (NetEase Intelligent)

2

Whether at MSRA or FAIR, Kaiming He has consistently demonstrated steady and excellent performance, overcoming challenges one after another and delivering many significant research achievements to academia. (Tsinghua Alumni Association)

1

Kaiming He is one of the most influential figures in the field of deep learning, having published a series of highly impactful papers in computer vision and deep learning, and is a recipient of multiple prestigious awards in the field. (Tsinghua University News Network)

23

"He peaked as soon as he debuted" is a true portrayal of Kaiming He. He achieved fame at a young age, yet continues to dedicate himself to research, consistently delivering new surprises. (Xinmin Evening News)

27
